finding_results.notes: Cliff now sets the law-office envelope aside without reading any beneficiary information. His leverage logic and later inference about Martin protecting Tommy rest on the fire document, Tommy's behavior, and Martin's long protection pattern, not on the will.
The chapter now states Cliff's leverage logic once, then uses the ending to introduce a new pressure source: Tommy reads as volatile and the photos themselves feel riskier than Cliff first assumed.
Doug is characterized through withdrawal and disappearance rather than repeating the prior chapter's weight-loss and hoodie description.
Tommy keeps the white-knuckle behavioral tell, but the reused crew-cut and graying-temples description is gone.
Chapter-level punctuation was normalized. Verification after revision found 0 em-dash lines in 79 prose lines.
Sentence openings were varied across the chapter. Verification after revision found 15 rough 'He' openers across 350 heuristic sentence splits, approximately 4.29%.
The chapter now contains only two instances of the word 'something.'

Chapter 8 now uses a continuity-safe, origin-neutral description of the lake landscape print and no longer adds a new contradictory backstory. The broader four-chapter inconsistency cannot be fully resolved from this chapter-only pass.
